title: BF.MEXISTS
description: Checks whether one or more items exist in a Bloom Filter
complexity: O(k * n), where k is the number of hash functions and n is the number of items

params.description: The name of the Bloom filter
params.name: key

returns.description: List of booleans indicating if each item may exist

params.description: The name of the Bloom filter
params.name: key

params.description: The name of the Bloom filter
params.name: key

params.description: The name of the Bloom filter
params.name: key

params.description: The name of the Bloom filter
params.name: key

params.description: The name of the Bloom filter
params.name: key

nredisstack_async.signature: async Task<bool[]> MExistsAsync(RedisKey key, RedisValue[] items)

params.description: The name of the Bloom filter
params.name: $key

content:
Determines whether one or more items were added to a Bloom filter. This command is similar to [`BF.EXISTS`](), except that more than one item can be checked. ## Required arguments
key is key name for a Bloom filter.
item... One or more items to check.
## Examples {{< highlight bash >}} redis> BF.MADD bf item1 item2 1) (integer) 1 2) (integer) 1 redis> BF.MEXISTS bf item1 item2 item3 1) (integer) 1 2) (integer) 1 3) (integer) 0 {{< / highlight >}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Supported
| ✅ Flexible & Annual
✅ Free & Fixed | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="bf-mexists-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Array reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays" >}}) of [integer replies]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers" >}}), where `1` means that, with high probability, `item` was already added to the filter, and `0` means that `key` does not exist or that `item` was definitely not added to the filter. * [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: in these cases: invalid arguments, wrong key type, or when the key was not found. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Array reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays" >}}) of [boolean replies]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#booleans" >}}), where `true` means that, with high probability, `item` was already added to the filter, and `false` means that `key` does not exist or that `item` was definitely not added to the filter. * [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: in these cases: invalid arguments, wrong key type, or when the key was not found. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
redis> BF.MADD bf item1 item2
1) (integer) 1
2) (integer) 1
redis> BF.MEXISTS bf item1 item2 item3
1) (integer) 1
2) (integer) 1
3) (integer) 0
intro:
Determines whether one or more items were added to a Bloom filter.

This command is similar to [`BF.EXISTS`](), except that more than one item can be checked.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Determines whether one or more items were added to a Bloom filter.

This command is similar to [`BF.EXISTS`](), except that more than one item can be checked.

sections.title: Required arguments
sections.content:
key is key name for a Bloom filter.
item... One or more items to check.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{< highlight bash >}}
redis> BF.MADD bf item1 item2
1) (integer) 1
2) (integer) 1
redis> BF.MEXISTS bf item1 item2 item3
1) (integer) 1
2) (integer) 1
3) (integer) 0
{{< / highlight >}}

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="bf-mexists-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Array reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays" >}}) of [integer replies]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers" >}}), where `1` means that, with high probability, `item` was already added to the filter, and `0` means that `key` does not exist or that `item` was definitely not added to the filter.
* [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: in these cases: invalid arguments, wrong key type, or when the key was not found.

-tab-sep-

One of the following:
* [Array reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays" >}}) of [boolean replies]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#booleans" >}}), where `true` means that, with high probability, `item` was already added to the filter, and `false` means that `key` does not exist or that `item` was definitely not added to the filter.
* [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: in these cases: invalid arguments, wrong key type, or when the key was not found.

{{< /multitabs >}}
